# 3-Minute Demo Video Script — Cognivern on Canton

**Target length:** ~2:45–3:00  
**Audio source:** `.artifacts/demo-narration.aiff` generated by macOS `say`  
**Video source:** Playwright screen recording of `https://cognivern.vercel.app/sealed-bid`  
**Output:** `docs/demo-video.mp4`

## Narration (read by `say`)

> This is Cognivern — private sealed-bid vendor selection on Canton Network.
>
> Institutional RFPs and OTC desks leak pricing. We built a Daml contract where bids exist, but only the auctioneer can read the amounts, and the reveal is atomic.
>
> Here's the live product at cognivern dot vercel dot app slash sealed-bid. We will create a Canton-backed private RFP, submit three bids, and show that competitors cannot see each other's amounts.
>
> We create a new Canton round. The backend writes a SealedBidAuction contract to the ledger.
>
> Alice submits a sealed bid of $91,000. On Canton, the amount is visible only to the auctioneer.
>
> Bob bids $74,500.
>
> Charlie bids $108,000.
>
> Now we toggle the party view. As Alice, only Alice's bid is readable. As Bob, only Bob's. As the auctioneer, all three are visible. This is not our backend filter — it is the Daml participant node enforcing disclosure.
>
> The auctioneer closes bidding. And reveals the winner atomically. CloseAndReveal consumes every losing Bid in one ledger transaction and creates the AuctionResult.
>
> Bob wins at $74,500. The losing amounts are archived and never disclosed to competitors. That is structural privacy.
>
> Cognivern: institutional sealed-bid workflows, provably private on Canton. Visit the live demo and the public repo.

## Shot list / timing

| Time | Shot | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0:00–0:07 | Landing | Static on `/sealed-bid` list. |
| 0:07–0:23 | Problem context | Slow pan / still of the page, no clicks. |
| 0:23–0:45 | Create round | Click **New round**, fill description + category, click **Create round**. |
| 0:45–1:00 | Alice bid | Select Alice, enter $91,000, submit. |
| 1:00–1:15 | Bob bid | Select Bob, enter $74,500, submit. |
| 1:15–1:28 | Charlie bid | Select Charlie, enter $108,000, submit. |
| 1:28–1:58 | Party view | Click **Auctioneer**, **Alice**, **Bob**, **Charlie**, then back to **Auctioneer**. |
| 1:58–2:05 | Close round | Click **Close bidding**. |
| 2:05–2:20 | Reveal | Click **Reveal winner atomically**; wait for winner banner. |
| 2:20–3:00 | Closing hold | Hold on winner banner + privacy message while narration finishes. |

## Notes for re-recording on DevNet

Once the production backend is pointed at Canton DevNet:

1. Update the browser URL in `scripts/demo/record-demo-video.ts` if you want to show a different deployment.
2. Re-run `pnpm tsx scripts/demo/record-demo-video.ts`.
3. The script will create a fresh round, submit three bids, and capture the lifecycle on DevNet.
4. Update this script with the actual DevNet package ID / participant name before recording the final submission cut, or add an overlay card in post-production.

## Re-running

```bash
pnpm tsx scripts/demo/record-demo-video.ts
```

Requires macOS `say` and `ffmpeg`. The script is self-contained and overwrites `docs/demo-video.mp4`.
